title: carpinus londoniana
intro: Carpinus londoniana, also known as London planetree or London plane, is a deciduous tree native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Carpinus londoniana lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Carpinus londoniana can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and have also been used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
